# EduOS Cameroon

# Textbook Distribution Tracking System

## Complete Detailed Product, UX, Workflow, RBAC and Functional Specification --- 86 Screens

**Document status:** Detailed implementation baseline\
**Scope:** 86 unique functional screens across 13 modules\
**Design objective:** National-identity, heritage-aware,
government-grade enterprise system

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 1. Purpose

This specification defines the complete operational screen architecture
for the EduOS Cameroon Textbook Distribution Tracking System. It covers
users, responsibilities, permissions, end-to-end flows, page layouts,
fields, controls, tables, validations, states, business rules, audit
events, navigation and acceptance criteria.

## 2. Account Types and Responsibility Model

1.  **National Super Administrator** --- platform configuration,
    identity, access control and technical administration; no automatic
    operational approval authority.
2.  **National Programme Administrator** --- campaigns, national
    planning, allocation governance and national performance.
3.  **Procurement & Supply Officer** --- procurement orders, suppliers
    and inbound supply coordination.
4.  **National Warehouse Manager** --- central receiving, inventory
    control, picking, packing and dispatch supervision.
5.  **Regional / Delegation Officer** --- regional allocation,
    distribution oversight and escalation.
6.  **Regional / District Warehouse Officer** --- local stock,
    receiving, transfers and shipment execution.
7.  **Transport / Logistics Officer** --- vehicles, drivers, routes,
    trips, tracking and incidents.
8.  **School Head / Authorized Receiver** --- school receipt, counting,
    discrepancy reporting and proof of delivery.
9.  **Inspector / Field Verification Officer** --- independent physical
    verification and evidence.
10. **Auditor / Monitoring & Evaluation Officer** --- read-only
    oversight, reconciliation, analytics and audit.

### 2.1 High-Level Responsibility Matrix

  ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  Role                       Create      Review        Approve     Dispatch             Receive         Verify    Audit/Admin
  --------------- ----------------- ----------- -------------- ------------ ------------------- -------------- --------------
  National Super        Config only     Limited No operational           No                  No             No            Yes

  Officer                                        workflow only                                                        history

  Warehouse              operations              controls when                          receipt                       history
  Manager                                           authorized

  Officer                                        controls when                                                      oversight
                                                    authorized

  Officer                   records                   approval   authorized

  Officer                                        controls when     handover                                           history
                                                    authorized

  ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 3. Mandatory Separation of Duties

No person may control the complete custody lifecycle of the same
transaction. At minimum:

-   The creator of a high-risk transaction cannot be its sole final
    approver.
-   The dispatcher cannot act as the independent verifier.
-   The receiver cannot independently verify the same delivery.
-   Administrators cannot gain operational authority merely because they
    administer accounts.
-   Multiple assigned roles do not override conflict-of-interest rules.
-   Emergency overrides require explicit authorization, reason capture
    and enhanced audit review.

## 4. End-to-End System Flow

``` text
Enrollment / School Requirement Data
        ↓
Distribution Campaign
        ↓
Demand Calculation
        ↓
Allocation Draft
        ↓
Review and Approval
        ↓
Available Stock Check / Procurement
        ↓
Supplier Delivery
        ↓
Warehouse Receipt
        ↓
Receiving Inspection
        ↓
Inventory Posting
        ↓
Shipment Creation
        ↓
Picking
        ↓
Packing / Cartonization / Sealing
        ↓
Dispatch Approval
        ↓
Physical Handover and Dispatch
        ↓
Transport / Tracking
        ↓
Intermediate Warehouse Transfer (if required)
        ↓
School Arrival
        ↓
Physical Count and Condition Check
        ↓
Discrepancy Capture (if applicable)
        ↓
Digital Proof of Delivery
        ↓
Independent Verification
        ↓
Exception Investigation and Resolution
        ↓
Reconciliation, Analytics and Audit
```

## 5. Global Design and Layout Specification

### 5.1 Brand Colors

  Token            Hex         Primary use
  ---------------- ----------- -------------------------------------
  Heritage Green   `#0D5C3B`   Primary actions, brand emphasis
  Deep Forest      `#063A2A`   Sidebar, dark surfaces
  Cameroon Gold    `#D4A017`   Active indicators, official accents
  Ivory White      `#FCFBF7`   Main background
  Emerald          `#1E8E5A`   Positive operational state
  Navy             `#0A2F5A`   Information and selected analytics
  Light Gray       `#F4F5F7`   Secondary surfaces
  Border Gray      `#D9DFE6`   Dividers and borders
  Primary Text     `#202124`   Main text
  Secondary Text   `#5F6368`   Supporting text
  Error            `#D32F2F`   Critical/error state
  Warning          `#F9A825`   Warning state
  Info             `#1976D2`   Informational state

### 5.2 Typography

-   Primary font: **Inter**
-   Page title: **36px / 700**
-   Section title: **24px / 600**
-   Card title: **18--22px / 600**
-   Body: **16px / 400**
-   Table text: **14px / 400--500**
-   Caption/helper text: **12--14px**
-   KPI values: **32--40px / 700**

### 5.3 Layout

-   Desktop container: 1440px maximum.
-   Content grid: 12 columns.
-   Standard gutter: 24px.
-   Sidebar: 280px expanded; 72px collapsed.
-   Top navigation: 72--88px depending on context.
-   Standard content padding: 24--32px.
-   Cards: 20px radius; 24px internal padding.
-   Inputs: 48--52px height; 12px radius.
-   Buttons: 48--52px height; 14px radius.
-   Tables: 52--56px row height.
-   Heritage motifs: subtle, normally 5--10% opacity; never behind dense
    text or data.

### 5.4 Global Page Anatomy

1.  National identity / product shell.
2.  Role-scoped navigation.
3.  Breadcrumb.
4.  Page title and contextual description.
5.  Primary action zone.
6.  Filters/search where applicable.
7.  Main data/work area.
8.  Contextual detail drawer or modal where appropriate.
9.  Status feedback and notifications.
10. Audit-safe confirmation for sensitive actions.

### 5.5 Global States

Every applicable screen defines: - Initial loading. - Ready. - Empty. -
Filtered no-result. - Validation error. - Permission denied. -
Network/service failure. - Offline/degraded mode where supported. -
Submitted/processing. - Success. - Locked/read-only. -
Archived/historical where applicable.

## 6. Global Security, RBAC and Audit Rules

-   Authorization is enforced server-side.
-   Access is role-based and scope-based.
-   Scope may include organization, geography, warehouse, school,
    campaign and data classification.
-   URLs never grant access by themselves.
-   High-risk actions require re-authentication or step-up
    authentication when policy requires.
-   State-changing APIs use duplicate-submission protection.
-   Audit logs record actor, effective role, scope, timestamp, source
    context, record, action and state transition.
-   Passwords, OTPs, recovery tokens and secrets are never logged.
-   Posted inventory and custody history is append-only; corrections use
    controlled reversal/correction events.
-   File evidence retains provenance and access controls.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Module 1 --- Authentication & Account Management

## Screen 1 of 86 --- AUTH-01: Login

### A. Purpose

Securely authenticate a user and route them to the correct role-scoped
workspace.

### B. Primary users

**All users**

### C. Entry points

-   Relevant role dashboard.
-   Parent module list or work queue.
-   Authorized deep link from a notification, alert, report or linked
    transaction.
-   Related record navigation where the user's scope permits access.

### D. Page layout

-   **Header zone:** breadcrumb, page title, status/context and primary
    action.
-   **Control zone:** search, filters, scope selectors or workflow
    controls as applicable.
-   **Main work zone:** form, cards, table, map, chart, timeline or task
    workspace appropriate to this screen.
-   **Context zone:** related records, evidence, history, comments or
    alerts.
-   **Action zone:** primary action on the right; secondary actions
    grouped separately; destructive/irreversible actions visually
    separated.
-   **Responsive behavior:** filters collapse into a drawer on narrow
    screens; tables use priority columns plus expandable row details;
    critical actions remain reachable without horizontal scrolling.

### E. Core fields and data

-   **User identifier or official email**
-   **Password / recovery challenge as applicable**
-   **Remember/trusted-device control where policy permits**
-   **Security status and session metadata**
-   **Email / User ID**
-   **Password**
-   **Remember this device (policy-controlled)**

### F. Primary actions

-   **Submit or continue**
-   **Back to previous authentication step**
-   **Show/hide password where applicable**
-   **Request help or recovery**

### G. Detailed workflow

Enter credentials → client validation → server authentication →
account-status check → risk/MFA decision → AUTH-04 if required → resolve
role and scope → route to dashboard.

### H. Business rules

-   Authentication responses must resist account enumeration.
-   Privileged users require MFA according to policy.
-   Rate limiting and lockout controls apply.
-   Secrets are never logged or returned in plaintext.
-   The user's effective organizational and geographic scope is checked
    on every read and write operation.
-   A state transition is rejected when prerequisites are incomplete.
-   Historical records referenced by completed transactions cannot be
    silently deleted.
-   Sensitive overrides require a reason and are highlighted for audit
    review.

### I. Validation rules

-   Required fields are validated on both client and server.
-   IDs and references must resolve to active, authorized records.
-   Quantities must be whole non-negative values unless a specific unit
    model permits otherwise.
-   Dates must be logically ordered and respect campaign/workflow
    constraints.
-   Duplicate identifiers and duplicate submissions are rejected.
-   Files/evidence are validated for permitted type, size, malware
    scanning and access classification.
-   Concurrent edits use version checks or transaction locking to
    prevent silent overwrites.

### J. Permissions

-   **View:** only within assigned scope.
-   **Create:** only for roles explicitly authorized for this record
    type.
-   **Edit:** limited by record state; submitted/approved/posted records
    become restricted or read-only.
-   **Approve:** separate permission from create/edit.
-   **Delete:** generally prohibited for transactional records; use
    archive/cancel/reversal workflows.
-   **Export:** separately permissioned and audited where data
    sensitivity requires it.

### K. Table/list specification

This screen is primarily form/workflow based. Where a list is present,
it follows the global table standard with server-side authorization and
pagination.

### L. Status and error handling

-   Loading skeleton rather than layout shift.
-   Empty state explains why no records are present and shows an
    authorized next action.
-   Validation errors appear beside the affected field and in an
    accessible summary when multiple errors exist.
-   Permission failures do not expose hidden record details.
-   Service failures preserve unsent user input where safe.
-   Successful state changes show confirmation and the resulting
    record/status.
-   Locked records clearly explain why they are read-only and identify
    the next authorized workflow action.

### M. Audit events

-   Page/record access where required by policy.
-   Create, edit, submit, approve, reject, cancel, dispatch, receive,
    verify, resolve, export or configuration actions as applicable.
-   Actor ID, effective role, organizational/geographic scope,
    timestamp, record ID and action outcome.
-   Previous and new state for sensitive changes.
-   Reason/comment for rejection, override, adjustment, escalation and
    other exceptional actions.

### N. Notifications and integrations

-   Generate in-app notifications for assigned tasks, approvals,
    rejections, overdue items and critical exceptions as applicable.
-   Email/SMS/push channels are policy-controlled and never replace the
    authoritative in-system record.
-   Related modules receive events or state updates through controlled
    integration contracts.
-   External integrations must be idempotent, authenticated, observable
    and auditable.

### O. Accessibility

-   WCAG 2.2 AA target.
-   Full keyboard operation.
-   Visible focus state.
-   Programmatic labels and error association.
-   No status communicated by color alone.
-   Tables, dialogs, drawers, maps and charts require accessible
    alternatives or summaries.

### P. Acceptance criteria

-   The stated user can complete the screen's purpose end to end within
    authorized scope.
-   Unauthorized records and actions are blocked server-side.
-   Required business rules and state transitions are enforced.
-   Errors are clear and recoverable.
-   Successful changes are durable, traceable and visible in all related
    screens.
-   Audit events are complete and do not contain secrets.
-   Desktop, tablet and mobile behavior follows the approved design
    system.

-   Service failures preserve unsent user input where safe.
-   Successful state changes show confirmation and the resulting

-   Locked records clearly explain why they are read-only and identify

### M. Audit events

-   Create, edit, submit, approve, reject, cancel, dispatch, receive,

-   Actor ID, effective role, organizational/geographic scope,

### O. Accessibility

-   Tables, dialogs, drawers, maps and charts require accessible

### P. Acceptance criteria

-   Unauthorized records and actions are blocked server-side.

## Screen 3 of 86 --- AUTH-03: Reset Password

### B. Primary users

### C. Entry points

-   **Security status and session metadata**
-   **Recovery token/challenge (hidden)**
-   **New password**
-   **Confirm new password**
-   **Password-policy checklist**

### F. Primary actions

-   **Back to previous authentication step**
-   **Show/hide password where applicable**
-   **Request help or recovery**

password policy and breached-password controls where configured → save
new credential → invalidate token and configured sessions → confirmation
→ AUTH-01.

## Screen 4 of 86 --- AUTH-04: Multi-Factor Authentication

### A. Purpose

Complete a second-factor challenge before session establishment.

### B. Primary users

**Privileged/configured users**

### C. Entry points

-   Relevant role dashboard.
-   Parent module list or work queue.
-   Authorized deep link from a notification, alert, report or linked
    transaction.
-   Related record navigation where the user's scope permits access.

### D. Page layout

-   **Header zone:** breadcrumb, page title, status/context and primary
    action.
-   **Control zone:** search, filters, scope selectors or workflow
    controls as applicable.
-   **Main work zone:** form, cards, table, map, chart, timeline or task

-   **Remember/trusted-device control where policy permits**
-   **Security status and session metadata**
-   **Factor type**
-   **OTP / authenticator code**

### F. Primary actions

-   **Submit or continue**
-   **Back to previous authentication step**
-   **Show/hide password where applicable**
-   **Request help or recovery**

### G. Detailed workflow

Display approved factor challenge → user submits factor → verify →
retry/lockout decision → establish session → resolve workspace.
Backup/recovery paths follow policy and cannot weaken privileged-account
controls.

-   Authentication responses must resist account enumeration.
-   Privileged users require MFA according to policy.

-   Dates must be logically ordered and respect campaign/workflow

-   Duplicate identifiers and duplicate submissions are rejected.
-   Files/evidence are validated for permitted type, size, malware
    scanning and access classification.
-   Concurrent edits use version checks or transaction locking to
    prevent silent overwrites.

### J. Permissions

-   **View:** only within assigned scope.
-   **Create:** only for roles explicitly authorized for this record
    type.
-   **Edit:** limited by record state; submitted/approved/posted records
    become restricted or read-only.
-   **Approve:** separate permission from create/edit.
-   **Delete:** generally prohibited for transactional records; use
    archive/cancel/reversal workflows.
-   **Export:** separately permissioned and audited where data
    sensitivity requires it.

### K. Table/list specification

This screen is primarily form/workflow based. Where a list is present,
it follows the global table standard with server-side authorization and
pagination.

### L. Status and error handling

-   Loading skeleton rather than layout shift.
-   Empty state explains why no records are present and shows an
    authorized next action.
-   Validation errors appear beside the affected field and in an
    accessible summary when multiple errors exist.
-   Permission failures do not expose hidden record details.
-   Service failures preserve unsent user input where safe.
-   Successful state changes show confirmation and the resulting
    record/status.
-   Locked records clearly explain why they are read-only and identify
    the next authorized workflow action.

### M. Audit events

-   Page/record access where required by policy.
-   Create, edit, submit, approve, reject, cancel, dispatch, receive,

-   Actor ID, effective role, organizational/geographic scope,
    timestamp, record ID and action outcome.
-   Previous and new state for sensitive changes.
-   Reason/comment for rejection, override, adjustment, escalation and
    other exceptional actions.

### N. Notifications and integrations

-   Generate in-app notifications for assigned tasks, approvals,
    rejections, overdue items and critical exceptions as applicable.
-   Email/SMS/push channels are policy-controlled and never replace the
    authoritative in-system record.
-   Related modules receive events or state updates through controlled
    integration contracts.
-   External integrations must be idempotent, authenticated, observable
    and auditable.

### O. Accessibility

-   WCAG 2.2 AA target.
-   Full keyboard operation.
-   Visible focus state.
-   Programmatic labels and error association.
-   No status communicated by color alone.
-   Tables, dialogs, drawers, maps and charts require accessible
    alternatives or summaries.

### P. Acceptance criteria

-   The stated user can complete the screen's purpose end to end within
    authorized scope.
-   Unauthorized records and actions are blocked server-side.
-   Required business rules and state transitions are enforced.
-   Errors are clear and recoverable.
-   Successful changes are durable, traceable and visible in all related
    screens.
-   Audit events are complete and do not contain secrets.
-   Desktop, tablet and mobile behavior follows the approved design
    system.

## Screen 5 of 86 --- AUTH-05: My Profile

### A. Purpose

View and update permitted personal and contact information.

### B. Primary users

**All authenticated users**

### C. Entry points

-   Relevant role dashboard.
-   Parent module list or work queue.
-   Authorized deep link from a notification, alert, report or linked
    transaction.
-   Related record navigation where the user's scope permits access.

### D. Page layout

-   **Header zone:** breadcrumb, page title, status/context and primary
    action.
-   **Control zone:** search, filters, scope selectors or workflow
    controls as applicable.
-   **Main work zone:** form, cards, table, map, chart, timeline or task
    workspace appropriate to this screen.
-   **Context zone:** related records, evidence, history, comments or
    alerts.
-   **Action zone:** primary action on the right; secondary actions
    grouped separately; destructive/irreversible actions visually
    separated.
-   **Responsive behavior:** filters collapse into a drawer on narrow
    screens; tables use priority columns plus expandable row details;
    critical actions remain reachable without horizontal scrolling.

### E. Core fields and data

-   **User identifier or official email**
-   **Password / recovery challenge as applicable**
-   **Remember/trusted-device control where policy permits**
-   **Security status and session metadata**
-   **Full name**
-   **Official email**
-   **Phone where permitted**
-   **Preferred language**
-   **Read-only role/scope**

### F. Primary actions

-   **Submit or continue**
-   **Back to previous authentication step**
-   **Show/hide password where applicable**
-   **Request help or recovery**

### G. Detailed workflow

Load own profile → edit permitted fields → validate → save → audit
sensitive changes. Role, scope and privilege fields are read-only here.

### H. Business rules

-   Authentication responses must resist account enumeration.
-   Privileged users require MFA according to policy.
-   Rate limiting and lockout controls apply.
-   Secrets are never logged or returned in plaintext.
-   The user's effective organizational and geographic scope is checked
    on every read and write operation.
-   A state transition is rejected when prerequisites are incomplete.
-   Historical records referenced by completed transactions cannot be
    silently deleted.
-   Sensitive overrides require a reason and are highlighted for audit
    review.

### I. Validation rules

-   Required fields are validated on both client and server.
-   IDs and references must resolve to active, authorized records.
-   Quantities must be whole non-negative values unless a specific unit
    model permits otherwise.
-   Dates must be logically ordered and respect campaign/workflow
    constraints.
-   Duplicate identifiers and duplicate submissions are rejected.
-   Files/evidence are validated for permitted type, size, malware
    scanning and access classification.
-   Concurrent edits use version checks or transaction locking to
    prevent silent overwrites.

### J. Permissions

-   **View:** only within assigned scope.
-   **Create:** only for roles explicitly authorized for this record
    type.
-   **Edit:** limited by record state; submitted/approved/posted records
    become restricted or read-only.
-   **Approve:** separate permission from create/edit.
-   **Delete:** generally prohibited for transactional records; use
    archive/cancel/reversal workflows.
-   **Export:** separately permissioned and audited where data
    sensitivity requires it.

### K. Table/list specification

This screen is primarily form/workflow based. Where a list is present,
it follows the global table standard with server-side authorization and
pagination.

### L. Status and error handling

-   Loading skeleton rather than layout shift.
-   Empty state explains why no records are present and shows an
    authorized next action.
-   Validation errors appear beside the affected field and in an
    accessible summary when multiple errors exist.
-   Permission failures do not expose hidden record details.
-   Service failures preserve unsent user input where safe.
-   Successful state changes show confirmation and the resulting
    record/status.
-   Locked records clearly explain why they are read-only and identify
    the next authorized workflow action.

### M. Audit events

-   Page/record access where required by policy.
-   Create, edit, submit, approve, reject, cancel, dispatch, receive,
    verify, resolve, export or configuration actions as applicable.
-   Actor ID, effective role, organizational/geographic scope,
    timestamp, record ID and action outcome.
-   Previous and new state for sensitive changes.
-   Reason/comment for rejection, override, adjustment, escalation and
    other exceptional actions.

### N. Notifications and integrations

-   Generate in-app notifications for assigned tasks, approvals,
    rejections, overdue items and critical exceptions as applicable.
-   Email/SMS/push channels are policy-controlled and never replace the
    authoritative in-system record.
-   Related modules receive events or state updates through controlled
    integration contracts.
-   External integrations must be idempotent, authenticated, observable
    and auditable.

### O. Accessibility

-   WCAG 2.2 AA target.
-   Full keyboard operation.
-   Visible focus state.
-   Programmatic labels and error association.
-   No status communicated by color alone.
-   Tables, dialogs, drawers, maps and charts require accessible
    alternatives or summaries.

### P. Acceptance criteria

-   The stated user can complete the screen's purpose end to end within
    authorized scope.
-   Unauthorized records and actions are blocked server-side.
-   Required business rules and state transitions are enforced.
-   Errors are clear and recoverable.
-   Successful changes are durable, traceable and visible in all related
    screens.
-   Audit events are complete and do not contain secrets.
-   Desktop, tablet and mobile behavior follows the approved design
    system.

## Screen 6 of 86 --- AUTH-06: Security & Sessions

### A. Purpose

-   **Device/browser**
-   **IP/risk metadata**

# Module 2 --- Role-Based Dashboards

## Screen 7 of 86 --- DASH-01: National Command Dashboard

### A. Purpose

Provide national command visibility over campaigns, stock, distribution,
coverage, exceptions and risks.

### B. Primary users

**National Programme Administrator**

### C. Entry points

-   Relevant role dashboard.
-   Parent module list or work queue.
-   Authorized deep link from a notification, alert, report or linked
    transaction.
-   Related record navigation where the user's scope permits access.

### D. Page layout

-   **Header zone:** breadcrumb, page title, status/context and primary
    action.
-   **Control zone:** search, filters, scope selectors or workflow
    controls as applicable.
-   **Main work zone:** form, cards, table, map, chart, timeline or task
    workspace appropriate to this screen.
-   **Context zone:** related records, evidence, history, comments or
    alerts.
-   **Action zone:** primary action on the right; secondary actions
    grouped separately; destructive/irreversible actions visually
    separated.
-   **Responsive behavior:** filters collapse into a drawer on narrow
    screens; tables use priority columns plus expandable row details;
    critical actions remain reachable without horizontal scrolling.

### E. Core fields and data

-   **Campaign selector**
-   **Date range**
-   **Geographic scope**
-   **Operational status**
-   **KPI cards**
-   **Alerts and priority tasks**

### F. Primary actions

-   **Filter and refresh**
-   **Drill into KPI**
-   **Open priority task**
-   **Open alert**
-   **Export authorized summary**

### G. Detailed workflow

Open National Command Dashboard → resolve role and scope → load
authorized data → review or enter information → validate → execute
permitted action → persist state change and audit event → update linked
screens and workflow queues.

### H. Business rules

-   KPIs must be calculated from authoritative transactional data.
-   Every metric respects the user's scope.
-   Dashboard counts must link to reconcilable detail views.
-   Stale-data timestamps must be visible.
-   The user's effective organizational and geographic scope is checked
    on every read and write operation.
-   A state transition is rejected when prerequisites are incomplete.
-   Historical records referenced by completed transactions cannot be
    silently deleted.
-   Sensitive overrides require a reason and are highlighted for audit
    review.

### I. Validation rules

-   Required fields are validated on both client and server.
-   IDs and references must resolve to active, authorized records.
-   Quantities must be whole non-negative values unless a specific unit
    model permits otherwise.
-   Dates must be logically ordered and respect campaign/workflow
    constraints.
-   Duplicate identifiers and duplicate submissions are rejected.
-   Files/evidence are validated for permitted type, size, malware
    scanning and access classification.
-   Concurrent edits use version checks or transaction locking to
    prevent silent overwrites.

### J. Permissions

-   **View:** only within assigned scope.
-   **Create:** only for roles explicitly authorized for this record
    type.
-   **Edit:** limited by record state; submitted/approved/posted records
    become restricted or read-only.
-   **Approve:** separate permission from create/edit.
-   **Delete:** generally prohibited for transactional records; use
    archive/cancel/reversal workflows.
-   **Export:** separately permissioned and audited where data
    sensitivity requires it.

### K. Table/list specification

  Priority                Item   Scope   Status   Age   Owner   Action
  ----------------------- ------ ------- -------- ----- ------- --------
  *Example/loaded data*   ...    ...     ...      ...   ...     ...

Table behavior: sortable permitted columns, server-side pagination for
large datasets, persistent filters where useful, row-level actions
constrained by permission, and accessible keyboard navigation.

### L. Status and error handling

-   Loading skeleton rather than layout shift.
-   Empty state explains why no records are present and shows an
    authorized next action.
-   Validation errors appear beside the affected field and in an
    accessible summary when multiple errors exist.
-   Permission failures do not expose hidden record details.
-   Service failures preserve unsent user input where safe.
-   Successful state changes show confirmation and the resulting
    record/status.
-   Locked records clearly explain why they are read-only and identify
    the next authorized workflow action.

### M. Audit events

-   Page/record access where required by policy.
-   Create, edit, submit, approve, reject, cancel, dispatch, receive,
    verify, resolve, export or configuration actions as applicable.
-   Actor ID, effective role, organizational/geographic scope,
    timestamp, record ID and action outcome.
-   Previous and new state for sensitive changes.
-   Reason/comment for rejection, override, adjustment, escalation and
    other exceptional actions.

### N. Notifications and integrations

-   Generate in-app notifications for assigned tasks, approvals,
    rejections, overdue items and critical exceptions as applicable.
-   Email/SMS/push channels are policy-controlled and never replace the
    authoritative in-system record.
-   Related modules receive events or state updates through controlled
    integration contracts.
-   External integrations must be idempotent, authenticated, observable
    and auditable.

### O. Accessibility

-   WCAG 2.2 AA target.
-   Full keyboard operation.
-   Visible focus state.
-   Programmatic labels and error association.
-   No status communicated by color alone.
-   Tables, dialogs, drawers, maps and charts require accessible
    alternatives or summaries.

### P. Acceptance criteria

-   The stated user can complete the screen's purpose end to end within
    authorized scope.
-   Unauthorized records and actions are blocked server-side.
-   Required business rules and state transitions are enforced.
-   Errors are clear and recoverable.
-   Successful changes are durable, traceable and visible in all related
    screens.
-   Audit events are complete and do not contain secrets.
-   Desktop, tablet and mobile behavior follows the approved design
    system.

-   Successful changes are durable, traceable and visible in all related
    screens.
-   Audit events are complete and do not contain secrets.
-   Desktop, tablet and mobile behavior follows the approved design
    system.

## Screen 10 of 86 --- DASH-04: Regional Oversight Dashboard

### A. Purpose

Monitor regional allocation, inventory, deliveries, school coverage and
exceptions.

### B. Primary users

**Regional Officer**

### C. Entry points

-   Relevant role dashboard.
-   Parent module list or work queue.
-   Authorized deep link from a notification, alert, report or linked
    transaction.
-   Related record navigation where the user's scope permits access.

### D. Page layout

-   **Header zone:** breadcrumb, page title, status/context and primary
    action.
-   **Control zone:** search, filters, scope selectors or workflow
    controls as applicable.
-   **Main work zone:** form, cards, table, map, chart, timeline or task
    workspace appropriate to this screen.
-   **Context zone:** related records, evidence, history, comments or
    alerts.
-   **Action zone:** primary action on the right; secondary actions
    grouped separately; destructive/irreversible actions visually
    separated.
-   **Responsive behavior:** filters collapse into a drawer on narrow
    screens; tables use priority columns plus expandable row details;
    critical actions remain reachable without horizontal scrolling.

### E. Core fields and data

-   **Campaign selector**
-   **Date range**
-   **Geographic scope**
-   **Operational status**
-   **KPI cards**
-   **Alerts and priority tasks**

### F. Primary actions

-   **Filter and refresh**
-   **Drill into KPI**
-   **Open priority task**
-   **Open alert**
-   **Export authorized summary**

### G. Detailed workflow

Open Regional Oversight Dashboard → resolve role and scope → load
authorized data → review or enter information → validate → execute
permitted action → persist state change and audit event → update linked
screens and workflow queues.

### H. Business rules

-   KPIs must be calculated from authoritative transactional data.
-   Every metric respects the user's scope.
-   Dashboard counts must link to reconcilable detail views.
-   Stale-data timestamps must be visible.
-   The user's effective organizational and geographic scope is checked
    on every read and write operation.
-   A state transition is rejected when prerequisites are incomplete.
-   Historical records referenced by completed transactions cannot be
    silently deleted.
-   Sensitive overrides require a reason and are highlighted for audit
    review.

### I. Validation rules

-   Required fields are validated on both client and server.
-   IDs and references must resolve to active, authorized records.
-   Quantities must be whole non-negative values unless a specific unit
    model permits otherwise.
-   Dates must be logically ordered and respect campaign/workflow
    constraints.
-   Duplicate identifiers and duplicate submissions are rejected.
-   Files/evidence are validated for permitted type, size, malware
    scanning and access classification.
-   Concurrent edits use version checks or transaction locking to
    prevent silent overwrites.

### J. Permissions

-   **View:** only within assigned scope.
-   **Create:** only for roles explicitly authorized for this record
    type.
-   **Edit:** limited by record state; submitted/approved/posted records
    become restricted or read-only.
-   **Approve:** separate permission from create/edit.
-   **Delete:** generally prohibited for transactional records; use
    archive/cancel/reversal workflows.
-   **Export:** separately permissioned and audited where data
    sensitivity requires it.

### K. Table/list specification

  Priority                Item   Scope   Status   Age   Owner   Action
  ----------------------- ------ ------- -------- ----- ------- --------
  *Example/loaded data*   ...    ...     ...      ...   ...     ...

Table behavior: sortable permitted columns, server-side pagination for
large datasets, persistent filters where useful, row-level actions
constrained by permission, and accessible keyboard navigation.

### L. Status and error handling

-   Loading skeleton rather than layout shift.
-   Empty state explains why no records are present and shows an
    authorized next action.
-   Validation errors appear beside the affected field and in an
    accessible summary when multiple errors exist.
-   Permission failures do not expose hidden record details.
-   Service failures preserve unsent user input where safe.
-   Successful state changes show confirmation and the resulting
    record/status.
-   Locked records clearly explain why they are read-only and identify
    the next authorized workflow action.

### M. Audit events

-   Page/record access where required by policy.
-   Create, edit, submit, approve, reject, cancel, dispatch, receive,
    verify, resolve, export or configuration actions as applicable.
-   Actor ID, effective role, organizational/geographic scope,
    timestamp, record ID and action outcome.
-   Previous and new state for sensitive changes.
-   Reason/comment for rejection, override, adjustment, escalation and
    other exceptional actions.

### N. Notifications and integrations

-   Generate in-app notifications for assigned tasks, approvals,
    rejections, overdue items and critical exceptions as applicable.
-   Email/SMS/push channels are policy-controlled and never replace the
    authoritative in-system record.
-   Related modules receive events or state updates through controlled
    integration contracts.
-   External integrations must be idempotent, authenticated, observable
    and auditable.

### O. Accessibility

-   WCAG 2.2 AA target.
-   Full keyboard operation.
-   Visible focus state.
-   Programmatic labels and error association.
-   No status communicated by color alone.
-   Tables, dialogs, drawers, maps and charts require accessible
    alternatives or summaries.

### P. Acceptance criteria

-   The stated user can complete the screen's purpose end to end within
    authorized scope.
-   Unauthorized records and actions are blocked server-side.
-   Required business rules and state transitions are enforced.
-   Errors are clear and recoverable.
-   Successful changes are durable, traceable and visible in all related
    screens.
-   Audit events are complete and do not contain secrets.
-   Desktop, tablet and mobile behavior follows the approved design
    system.

-   **Header zone:** breadcrumb, page title, status/context and primary
    action.
-   **Control zone:** search, filters, scope selectors or workflow
    controls as applicable.
-   **Main work zone:** form, cards, table, map, chart, timeline or task
    workspace appropriate to this screen.
-   **Context zone:** related records, evidence, history, comments or
    alerts.
-   **Action zone:** primary action on the right; secondary actions
    grouped separately; destructive/irreversible actions visually
    separated.
-   **Responsive behavior:** filters collapse into a drawer on narrow
    screens; tables use priority columns plus expandable row details;
    critical actions remain reachable without horizontal scrolling.

### E. Core fields and data

-   **Textbook title**
-   **Subject**
-   **Education level/class**
-   **Edition**
-   **Language**
-   **Publisher**
-   **ISBN/internal identifier**
-   **Approval status**
-   **Effective/retirement dates**

### F. Primary actions

-   **Search/filter**
-   **View details**
-   **Create/edit when authorized**
-   **Map curriculum**
-   **Manage identifiers**
-   **Archive/retire with reason**

### G. Detailed workflow

Open Textbook Catalogue → resolve role and scope → load authorized data
→ review or enter information → validate → execute permitted action →
persist state change and audit event → update linked screens and
workflow queues.

### H. Business rules

-   Identifiers must be unique within their identifier type.
-   Retired textbooks remain historically traceable.
-   Edition changes do not overwrite prior distribution history.
-   Only approved records are eligible for new allocations.
-   The user's effective organizational and geographic scope is checked
    on every read and write operation.
-   A state transition is rejected when prerequisites are incomplete.
-   Historical records referenced by completed transactions cannot be
    silently deleted.
-   Sensitive overrides require a reason and are highlighted for audit
    review.

### I. Validation rules

-   Required fields are validated on both client and server.
-   IDs and references must resolve to active, authorized records.
-   Quantities must be whole non-negative values unless a specific unit
    model permits otherwise.
-   Dates must be logically ordered and respect campaign/workflow
    constraints.
-   Duplicate identifiers and duplicate submissions are rejected.
-   Files/evidence are validated for permitted type, size, malware
    scanning and access classification.
-   Concurrent edits use version checks or transaction locking to
    prevent silent overwrites.

### J. Permissions

-   **View:** only within assigned scope.
-   **Create:** only for roles explicitly authorized for this record
    type.
-   **Edit:** limited by record state; submitted/approved/posted records
    become restricted or read-only.
-   **Approve:** separate permission from create/edit.
-   **Delete:** generally prohibited for transactional records; use
    archive/cancel/reversal workflows.
-   **Export:** separately permissioned and audited where data
    sensitivity requires it.

### K. Table/list specification

  ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  Code              Title   Subject   Level   Edition   Language   Status   Action
  ----------------- ------- --------- ------- --------- ---------- -------- --------
  *Example/loaded   ...     ...       ...     ...       ...        ...      ...
  data*                                                                     

  ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Table behavior: sortable permitted columns, server-side pagination for
large datasets, persistent filters where useful, row-level actions
constrained by permission, and accessible keyboard navigation.

### L. Status and error handling

-   Loading skeleton rather than layout shift.
-   Empty state explains why no records are present and shows an
    authorized next action.
-   Validation errors appear beside the affected field and in an
    accessible summary when multiple errors exist.
-   Permission failures do not expose hidden record details.
-   Service failures preserve unsent user input where safe.
-   Successful state changes show confirmation and the resulting
    record/status.
-   Locked records clearly explain why they are read-only and identify
    the next authorized workflow action.

### M. Audit events

-   Page/record access where required by policy.
-   Create, edit, submit, approve, reject, cancel, dispatch, receive,
    verify, resolve, export or configuration actions as applicable.
-   Actor ID, effective role, organizational/geographic scope,
    timestamp, record ID and action outcome.
-   Previous and new state for sensitive changes.
-   Reason/comment for rejection, override, adjustment, escalation and
    other exceptional actions.

### N. Notifications and integrations

-   Generate in-app notifications for assigned tasks, approvals,
    rejections, overdue items and critical exceptions as applicable.
-   Email/SMS/push channels are policy-controlled and never replace the
    authoritative in-system record.
-   Related modules receive events or state updates through controlled
    integration contracts.
-   External integrations must be idempotent, authenticated, observable
    and auditable.

### O. Accessibility

-   WCAG 2.2 AA target.
-   Full keyboard operation.
-   Visible focus state.
-   Programmatic labels and error association.
-   No status communicated by color alone.
-   Tables, dialogs, drawers, maps and charts require accessible
    alternatives or summaries.

### P. Acceptance criteria

-   The stated user can complete the screen's purpose end to end within
    authorized scope.
-   Unauthorized records and actions are blocked server-side.
-   Required business rules and state transitions are enforced.
-   Errors are clear and recoverable.
-   Successful changes are durable, traceable and visible in all related
    screens.
-   Audit events are complete and do not contain secrets.
-   Desktop, tablet and mobile behavior follows the approved design
    system.

    transaction.
-   Related record navigation where the user's scope permits access.

### D. Page layout

-   **Header zone:** breadcrumb, page title, status/context and primary
    action.
-   **Control zone:** search, filters, scope selectors or workflow
    controls as applicable.
-   **Main work zone:** form, cards, table, map, chart, timeline or task
    workspace appropriate to this screen.
-   **Context zone:** related records, evidence, history, comments or
    alerts.
-   **Action zone:** primary action on the right; secondary actions
    grouped separately; destructive/irreversible actions visually
    separated.
-   **Responsive behavior:** filters collapse into a drawer on narrow
    screens; tables use priority columns plus expandable row details;
    critical actions remain reachable without horizontal scrolling.

### E. Core fields and data

-   **Textbook title**
-   **Subject**
-   **Education level/class**
-   **Edition**
-   **Language**
-   **Publisher**
-   **ISBN/internal identifier**
-   **Approval status**
-   **Effective/retirement dates**

### F. Primary actions

-   **Search/filter**
-   **View details**
-   **Create/edit when authorized**
-   **Map curriculum**
-   **Manage identifiers**
-   **Archive/retire with reason**

### G. Detailed workflow

Open Textbook Batch Management → resolve role and scope → load
authorized data → review or enter information → validate → execute
permitted action → persist state change and audit event → update linked
screens and workflow queues.

### H. Business rules

-   Identifiers must be unique within their identifier type.
-   Retired textbooks remain historically traceable.
-   Edition changes do not overwrite prior distribution history.
-   Only approved records are eligible for new allocations.
-   The user's effective organizational and geographic scope is checked
    on every read and write operation.
-   A state transition is rejected when prerequisites are incomplete.
-   Historical records referenced by completed transactions cannot be
    silently deleted.
-   Sensitive overrides require a reason and are highlighted for audit
    review.

### I. Validation rules

-   Required fields are validated on both client and server.
-   IDs and references must resolve to active, authorized records.
-   Quantities must be whole non-negative values unless a specific unit
    model permits otherwise.
-   Dates must be logically ordered and respect campaign/workflow
    constraints.
-   Duplicate identifiers and duplicate submissions are rejected.
-   Files/evidence are validated for permitted type, size, malware
    scanning and access classification.
-   Concurrent edits use version checks or transaction locking to
    prevent silent overwrites.

### J. Permissions

-   **View:** only within assigned scope.
-   **Create:** only for roles explicitly authorized for this record
    type.
-   **Edit:** limited by record state; submitted/approved/posted records
    become restricted or read-only.
-   **Approve:** separate permission from create/edit.
-   **Delete:** generally prohibited for transactional records; use
    archive/cancel/reversal workflows.
-   **Export:** separately permissioned and audited where data
    sensitivity requires it.

### K. Table/list specification

  ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  Code              Title   Subject   Level   Edition   Language   Status   Action
  ----------------- ------- --------- ------- --------- ---------- -------- --------
  *Example/loaded   ...     ...       ...     ...       ...        ...      ...
  data*                                                                     

  ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Table behavior: sortable permitted columns, server-side pagination for
large datasets, persistent filters where useful, row-level actions
constrained by permission, and accessible keyboard navigation.

### L. Status and error handling

-   Loading skeleton rather than layout shift.
-   Empty state explains why no records are present and shows an
    authorized next action.
-   Validation errors appear beside the affected field and in an
    accessible summary when multiple errors exist.
-   Permission failures do not expose hidden record details.
-   Service failures preserve unsent user input where safe.
-   Successful state changes show confirmation and the resulting
    record/status.
-   Locked records clearly explain why they are read-only and identify
    the next authorized workflow action.

### M. Audit events

-   Page/record access where required by policy.
-   Create, edit, submit, approve, reject, cancel, dispatch, receive,
    verify, resolve, export or configuration actions as applicable.
-   Actor ID, effective role, organizational/geographic scope,
    timestamp, record ID and action outcome.
-   Previous and new state for sensitive changes.
-   Reason/comment for rejection, override, adjustment, escalation and
    other exceptional actions.

### N. Notifications and integrations

-   Generate in-app notifications for assigned tasks, approvals,
    rejections, overdue items and critical exceptions as applicable.
-   Email/SMS/push channels are policy-controlled and never replace the
    authoritative in-system record.
-   Related modules receive events or state updates through controlled
    integration contracts.
-   External integrations must be idempotent, authenticated, observable
    and auditable.

### O. Accessibility

-   WCAG 2.2 AA target.
-   Full keyboard operation.
-   Visible focus state.
-   Programmatic labels and error association.
-   No status communicated by color alone.
-   Tables, dialogs, drawers, maps and charts require accessible
    alternatives or summaries.

### P. Acceptance criteria

-   The stated user can complete the screen's purpose end to end within
    authorized scope.
-   Unauthorized records and actions are blocked server-side.
-   Required business rules and state transitions are enforced.
-   Errors are clear and recoverable.
-   Successful changes are durable, traceable and visible in all related
    screens.
-   Audit events are complete and do not contain secrets.
-   Desktop, tablet and mobile behavior follows the approved design
    system.

## Screen 21 of 86 --- PLAN-01: Distribution Campaigns

### A. Purpose

### B. Primary users

-   Parent module list or work queue.

    transaction.

Open Distribution Campaigns → resolve role and scope → load authorized
data → review or enter information → validate → execute permitted action
→ persist state change and audit event → update linked screens and
workflow queues.

### H. Business rules

-   Demand calculations retain source data and formula version.
-   Allocation cannot exceed available plus explicitly approved expected
    stock.
-   Approved allocation versions are immutable; amendments create a new
    version.
-   Equity and coverage exceptions require documented justification.
-   The user's effective organizational and geographic scope is checked
    on every read and write operation.
-   A state transition is rejected when prerequisites are incomplete.
-   Historical records referenced by completed transactions cannot be
    silently deleted.
-   Sensitive overrides require a reason and are highlighted for audit
    review.

-   Required fields are validated on both client and server.
-   IDs and references must resolve to active, authorized records.

-   Dates must be logically ordered and respect campaign/workflow
    constraints.
-   Duplicate identifiers and duplicate submissions are rejected.
-   Files/evidence are validated for permitted type, size, malware
    scanning and access classification.
-   Concurrent edits use version checks or transaction locking to
    prevent silent overwrites.

-   **View:** only within assigned scope.
-   **Create:** only for roles explicitly authorized for this record
    type.
-   **Edit:** limited by record state; submitted/approved/posted records
    become restricted or read-only.
-   **Approve:** separate permission from create/edit.
-   **Delete:** generally prohibited for transactional records; use
    archive/cancel/reversal workflows.
-   **Export:** separately permissioned and audited where data
    sensitivity requires it.

-   Required business rules and state transitions are enforced.
-   Errors are clear and recoverable.
-   Successful changes are durable, traceable and visible in all related
    screens.
-   Audit events are complete and do not contain secrets.
-   Desktop, tablet and mobile behavior follows the approved design
    system.

## Screen 25 of 86 --- PLAN-05: Allocation Workspace

### A. Purpose

Allocate available or expected stock across destinations.

### B. Primary users

**Planning Officers**

### C. Entry points

-   Relevant role dashboard.
-   Parent module list or work queue.
-   Authorized deep link from a notification, alert, report or linked
    transaction.
-   Related record navigation where the user's scope permits access.

### D. Page layout

-   **Header zone:** breadcrumb, page title, status/context and primary
    action.
-   **Control zone:** search, filters, scope selectors or workflow
    controls as applicable.
-   **Main work zone:** form, cards, table, map, chart, timeline or task
    workspace appropriate to this screen.
-   **Context zone:** related records, evidence, history, comments or
    alerts.
-   **Action zone:** primary action on the right; secondary actions
    grouped separately; destructive/irreversible actions visually
    separated.
-   **Responsive behavior:** filters collapse into a drawer on narrow
    screens; tables use priority columns plus expandable row details;
    critical actions remain reachable without horizontal scrolling.

### E. Core fields and data

-   **Campaign**
-   **Academic year**
-   **Geographic scope**
-   **School/destination**
-   **Enrollment source**
-   **Demand ratio**
-   **Calculated demand**
-   **Available/expected stock**
-   **Proposed allocation**
-   **Approved allocation**
-   **Status**

### F. Primary actions

-   **Calculate demand**
-   **Save draft**
-   **Run validation**
-   **Review anomalies**
-   **Submit**
-   **Approve/reject with reason**

### G. Detailed workflow

Load demand and stock constraints → allocate manually or by approved
rule → continuously validate totals and equity indicators → save draft
version → submit to PLAN-06.

### H. Business rules

-   Demand calculations retain source data and formula version.
-   Allocation cannot exceed available plus explicitly approved expected
    stock.
-   Approved allocation versions are immutable; amendments create a new
    version.
-   Equity and coverage exceptions require documented justification.
-   The user's effective organizational and geographic scope is checked
    on every read and write operation.
-   A state transition is rejected when prerequisites are incomplete.
-   Historical records referenced by completed transactions cannot be
    silently deleted.
-   Sensitive overrides require a reason and are highlighted for audit
    review.

### I. Validation rules

-   Required fields are validated on both client and server.
-   IDs and references must resolve to active, authorized records.
-   Quantities must be whole non-negative values unless a specific unit
    model permits otherwise.
-   Dates must be logically ordered and respect campaign/workflow
    constraints.
-   Duplicate identifiers and duplicate submissions are rejected.
-   Files/evidence are validated for permitted type, size, malware
    scanning and access classification.
-   Concurrent edits use version checks or transaction locking to
    prevent silent overwrites.

### J. Permissions

-   **View:** only within assigned scope.
-   **Create:** only for roles explicitly authorized for this record
    type.
-   **Edit:** limited by record state; submitted/approved/posted records
    become restricted or read-only.
-   **Approve:** separate permission from create/edit.
-   **Delete:** generally prohibited for transactional records; use
    archive/cancel/reversal workflows.
-   **Export:** separately permissioned and audited where data
    sensitivity requires it.

### K. Table/list specification

  ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  Destination       Enrollment   Demand   Available   Proposed   Approved   Coverage % Status
  ----------------- ------------ -------- ----------- ---------- ---------- ---------- --------
  *Example/loaded   ...          ...      ...         ...        ...        ...        ...
  data*                                                                                

  ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Table behavior: sortable permitted columns, server-side pagination for
large datasets, persistent filters where useful, row-level actions
constrained by permission, and accessible keyboard navigation.

### L. Status and error handling

-   Loading skeleton rather than layout shift.
-   Empty state explains why no records are present and shows an
    authorized next action.
-   Validation errors appear beside the affected field and in an
    accessible summary when multiple errors exist.
-   Permission failures do not expose hidden record details.
-   Service failures preserve unsent user input where safe.
-   Successful state changes show confirmation and the resulting
    record/status.
-   Locked records clearly explain why they are read-only and identify
    the next authorized workflow action.

### M. Audit events

-   Page/record access where required by policy.
-   Create, edit, submit, approve, reject, cancel, dispatch, receive,
    verify, resolve, export or configuration actions as applicable.
-   Actor ID, effective role, organizational/geographic scope,
    timestamp, record ID and action outcome.
-   Previous and new state for sensitive changes.
-   Reason/comment for rejection, override, adjustment, escalation and
    other exceptional actions.

### N. Notifications and integrations

-   Generate in-app notifications for assigned tasks, approvals,
    rejections, overdue items and critical exceptions as applicable.
-   Email/SMS/push channels are policy-controlled and never replace the
    authoritative in-system record.
-   Related modules receive events or state updates through controlled
    integration contracts.
-   External integrations must be idempotent, authenticated, observable
    and auditable.

### O. Accessibility

-   WCAG 2.2 AA target.
-   Full keyboard operation.
-   Visible focus state.
-   Programmatic labels and error association.
-   No status communicated by color alone.
-   Tables, dialogs, drawers, maps and charts require accessible
    alternatives or summaries.

### P. Acceptance criteria

-   The stated user can complete the screen's purpose end to end within
    authorized scope.
-   Unauthorized records and actions are blocked server-side.
-   Required business rules and state transitions are enforced.
-   Errors are clear and recoverable.
-   Successful changes are durable, traceable and visible in all related
    screens.
-   Audit events are complete and do not contain secrets.
-   Desktop, tablet and mobile behavior follows the approved design
    system.

## Screen 29 of 86 --- PROC-01: Procurement Overview

    integration contracts.
-   External integrations must be idempotent, authenticated, observable
    and auditable.

### O. Accessibility

-   WCAG 2.2 AA target.
-   Full keyboard operation.
-   Visible focus state.
-   Programmatic labels and error association.
-   No status communicated by color alone.
-   Tables, dialogs, drawers, maps and charts require accessible
    alternatives or summaries.

### P. Acceptance criteria

-   The stated user can complete the screen's purpose end to end within
    authorized scope.
-   Unauthorized records and actions are blocked server-side.
-   Required business rules and state transitions are enforced.
-   Errors are clear and recoverable.
-   Successful changes are durable, traceable and visible in all related
    screens.
-   Audit events are complete and do not contain secrets.
-   Desktop, tablet and mobile behavior follows the approved design
    system.

## Screen 30 of 86 --- PROC-02: Procurement Orders

### A. Purpose

List, filter and manage textbook procurement/supply orders.

### B. Primary users

**Procurement users**

### C. Entry points

-   Relevant role dashboard.
-   Parent module list or work queue.
-   Authorized deep link from a notification, alert, report or linked
    transaction.
-   Related record navigation where the user's scope permits access.

### D. Page layout

-   **Header zone:** breadcrumb, page title, status/context and primary
    action.
-   **Control zone:** search, filters, scope selectors or workflow
    controls as applicable.
-   **Main work zone:** form, cards, table, map, chart, timeline or task
    workspace appropriate to this screen.
-   **Context zone:** related records, evidence, history, comments or
    alerts.
-   **Action zone:** primary action on the right; secondary actions
    grouped separately; destructive/irreversible actions visually
    separated.
-   **Responsive behavior:** filters collapse into a drawer on narrow
    screens; tables use priority columns plus expandable row details;
    critical actions remain reachable without horizontal scrolling.

### E. Core fields and data

-   **Order number**
-   **Supplier**
-   **Contract/reference**
-   **Textbook/batch**
-   **Ordered quantity**
-   **Unit/total value where authorized**
-   **Expected delivery date**
-   **Delivered/accepted/rejected quantity**
-   **Status**
-   **Documents**

### F. Primary actions

-   **Create/update order**
-   **Submit/review**
-   **Link expected delivery**
-   **Record supplier milestone**
-   **Verify delivery**
-   **Export**

### G. Detailed workflow

Open Procurement Orders → resolve role and scope → load authorized data
→ review or enter information → validate → execute permitted action →
persist state change and audit event → update linked screens and
workflow queues.

### H. Business rules

-   Order lines reference approved textbook records.
-   Received quantities reconcile to order lines or approved variance.
-   Supplier performance is derived from completed transactions.
-   Commercially sensitive fields are permission-restricted.
-   The user's effective organizational and geographic scope is checked
    on every read and write operation.
-   A state transition is rejected when prerequisites are incomplete.
-   Historical records referenced by completed transactions cannot be
    silently deleted.
-   Sensitive overrides require a reason and are highlighted for audit
    review.

### I. Validation rules

-   Required fields are validated on both client and server.
-   IDs and references must resolve to active, authorized records.
-   Quantities must be whole non-negative values unless a specific unit
    model permits otherwise.
-   Dates must be logically ordered and respect campaign/workflow
    constraints.
-   Duplicate identifiers and duplicate submissions are rejected.
-   Files/evidence are validated for permitted type, size, malware
    scanning and access classification.
-   Concurrent edits use version checks or transaction locking to
    prevent silent overwrites.

### J. Permissions

-   **View:** only within assigned scope.
-   **Create:** only for roles explicitly authorized for this record
    type.
-   **Edit:** limited by record state; submitted/approved/posted records
    become restricted or read-only.
-   **Approve:** separate permission from create/edit.
-   **Delete:** generally prohibited for transactional records; use
    archive/cancel/reversal workflows.
-   **Export:** separately permissioned and audited where data
    sensitivity requires it.

### K. Table/list specification

  -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  Order             Supplier   Lines   Ordered   Expected   Delivered   Variance   Status
                                       Qty       Date                              
  ----------------- ---------- ------- --------- ---------- ----------- ---------- --------
  *Example/loaded   ...        ...     ...       ...        ...         ...        ...
  data*                                                                            

  -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Table behavior: sortable permitted columns, server-side pagination for
large datasets, persistent filters where useful, row-level actions
constrained by permission, and accessible keyboard navigation.

### L. Status and error handling

-   Loading skeleton rather than layout shift.
-   Empty state explains why no records are present and shows an
    authorized next action.
-   Validation errors appear beside the affected field and in an
    accessible summary when multiple errors exist.
-   Permission failures do not expose hidden record details.
-   Service failures preserve unsent user input where safe.
-   Successful state changes show confirmation and the resulting
    record/status.
-   Locked records clearly explain why they are read-only and identify
    the next authorized workflow action.

### M. Audit events

-   Page/record access where required by policy.
-   Create, edit, submit, approve, reject, cancel, dispatch, receive,
    verify, resolve, export or configuration actions as applicable.
-   Actor ID, effective role, organizational/geographic scope,
    timestamp, record ID and action outcome.
-   Previous and new state for sensitive changes.
-   Reason/comment for rejection, override, adjustment, escalation and
    other exceptional actions.

### N. Notifications and integrations

-   Generate in-app notifications for assigned tasks, approvals,
    rejections, overdue items and critical exceptions as applicable.
-   Email/SMS/push channels are policy-controlled and never replace the
    authoritative in-system record.
-   Related modules receive events or state updates through controlled
    integration contracts.
-   External integrations must be idempotent, authenticated, observable
    and auditable.

### O. Accessibility

-   WCAG 2.2 AA target.
-   Full keyboard operation.
-   Visible focus state.
-   Programmatic labels and error association.
-   No status communicated by color alone.
-   Tables, dialogs, drawers, maps and charts require accessible
    alternatives or summaries.

### P. Acceptance criteria

-   The stated user can complete the screen's purpose end to end within
    authorized scope.
-   Unauthorized records and actions are blocked server-side.
-   Required business rules and state transitions are enforced.
-   Errors are clear and recoverable.
-   Successful changes are durable, traceable and visible in all related
    screens.
-   Audit events are complete and do not contain secrets.
-   Desktop, tablet and mobile behavior follows the approved design
    system.

-   Reason/comment for rejection, override, adjustment, escalation and
    other exceptional actions.

### N. Notifications and integrations

-   Generate in-app notifications for assigned tasks, approvals,
    rejections, overdue items and critical exceptions as applicable.
-   Email/SMS/push channels are policy-controlled and never replace the
    authoritative in-system record.
-   Related modules receive events or state updates through controlled
    integration contracts.
-   External integrations must be idempotent, authenticated, observable
    and auditable.

### O. Accessibility

-   WCAG 2.2 AA target.
-   Full keyboard operation.
-   Visible focus state.
-   Programmatic labels and error association.
-   No status communicated by color alone.
-   Tables, dialogs, drawers, maps and charts require accessible
    alternatives or summaries.

### P. Acceptance criteria

-   The stated user can complete the screen's purpose end to end within
    authorized scope.
-   Unauthorized records and actions are blocked server-side.
-   Required business rules and state transitions are enforced.
-   Errors are clear and recoverable.
-   Successful changes are durable, traceable and visible in all related
    screens.
-   Audit events are complete and do not contain secrets.
-   Desktop, tablet and mobile behavior follows the approved design
    system.

## Screen 35 of 86 --- PROC-07: Supplier Delivery Verification

### A. Purpose

Reconcile ordered, delivered, inspected, accepted and rejected
quantities.

### B. Primary users

**Receiving/Procurement officers**

### C. Entry points

-   Relevant role dashboard.
-   Parent module list or work queue.
-   Authorized deep link from a notification, alert, report or linked
    transaction.
-   Related record navigation where the user's scope permits access.

### D. Page layout

-   **Header zone:** breadcrumb, page title, status/context and primary
    action.
-   **Control zone:** search, filters, scope selectors or workflow
    controls as applicable.
-   **Main work zone:** form, cards, table, map, chart, timeline or task
    workspace appropriate to this screen.
-   **Context zone:** related records, evidence, history, comments or
    alerts.
-   **Action zone:** primary action on the right; secondary actions
    grouped separately; destructive/irreversible actions visually
    separated.
-   **Responsive behavior:** filters collapse into a drawer on narrow
    screens; tables use priority columns plus expandable row details;
    critical actions remain reachable without horizontal scrolling.

### E. Core fields and data

-   **Order number**
-   **Supplier**
-   **Contract/reference**
-   **Textbook/batch**
-   **Ordered quantity**
-   **Unit/total value where authorized**
-   **Expected delivery date**
-   **Delivered/accepted/rejected quantity**
-   **Status**
-   **Documents**

### F. Primary actions

-   **Create/update order**
-   **Submit/review**
-   **Link expected delivery**
-   **Record supplier milestone**
-   **Verify delivery**
-   **Export**

### G. Detailed workflow

Open Supplier Delivery Verification → resolve role and scope → load
authorized data → review or enter information → validate → execute
permitted action → persist state change and audit event → update linked
screens and workflow queues.

### H. Business rules

-   Order lines reference approved textbook records.
-   Received quantities reconcile to order lines or approved variance.
-   Supplier performance is derived from completed transactions.
-   Commercially sensitive fields are permission-restricted.
-   The user's effective organizational and geographic scope is checked
    on every read and write operation.
-   A state transition is rejected when prerequisites are incomplete.
-   Historical records referenced by completed transactions cannot be
    silently deleted.
-   Sensitive overrides require a reason and are highlighted for audit
    review.

### I. Validation rules

-   Required fields are validated on both client and server.
-   IDs and references must resolve to active, authorized records.
-   Quantities must be whole non-negative values unless a specific unit
    model permits otherwise.
-   Dates must be logically ordered and respect campaign/workflow
    constraints.
-   Duplicate identifiers and duplicate submissions are rejected.
-   Files/evidence are validated for permitted type, size, malware
    scanning and access classification.
-   Concurrent edits use version checks or transaction locking to
    prevent silent overwrites.

### J. Permissions

-   **View:** only within assigned scope.
-   **Create:** only for roles explicitly authorized for this record
    type.
-   **Edit:** limited by record state; submitted/approved/posted records
    become restricted or read-only.
-   **Approve:** separate permission from create/edit.
-   **Delete:** generally prohibited for transactional records; use
    archive/cancel/reversal workflows.
-   **Export:** separately permissioned and audited where data
    sensitivity requires it.

### K. Table/list specification

  -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  Order             Supplier   Lines   Ordered   Expected   Delivered   Variance   Status
                                       Qty       Date                              
  ----------------- ---------- ------- --------- ---------- ----------- ---------- --------
  *Example/loaded   ...        ...     ...       ...        ...         ...        ...
  data*                                                                            

  -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Table behavior: sortable permitted columns, server-side pagination for
large datasets, persistent filters where useful, row-level actions
constrained by permission, and accessible keyboard navigation.

### L. Status and error handling

-   Loading skeleton rather than layout shift.
-   Empty state explains why no records are present and shows an
    authorized next action.
-   Validation errors appear beside the affected field and in an
    accessible summary when multiple errors exist.
-   Permission failures do not expose hidden record details.
-   Service failures preserve unsent user input where safe.
-   Successful state changes show confirmation and the resulting
    record/status.
-   Locked records clearly explain why they are read-only and identify
    the next authorized workflow action.

### M. Audit events

-   Page/record access where required by policy.
-   Create, edit, submit, approve, reject, cancel, dispatch, receive,
    verify, resolve, export or configuration actions as applicable.
-   Actor ID, effective role, organizational/geographic scope,
    timestamp, record ID and action outcome.
-   Previous and new state for sensitive changes.
-   Reason/comment for rejection, override, adjustment, escalation and
    other exceptional actions.

### N. Notifications and integrations

-   Generate in-app notifications for assigned tasks, approvals,
    rejections, overdue items and critical exceptions as applicable.
-   Email/SMS/push channels are policy-controlled and never replace the
    authoritative in-system record.
-   Related modules receive events or state updates through controlled
    integration contracts.
-   External integrations must be idempotent, authenticated, observable
    and auditable.

### O. Accessibility

-   WCAG 2.2 AA target.
-   Full keyboard operation.
-   Visible focus state.
-   Programmatic labels and error association.
-   No status communicated by color alone.
-   Tables, dialogs, drawers, maps and charts require accessible
    alternatives or summaries.

### P. Acceptance criteria

-   The stated user can complete the screen's purpose end to end within
    authorized scope.
-   Unauthorized records and actions are blocked server-side.
-   Required business rules and state transitions are enforced.
-   Errors are clear and recoverable.
-   Successful changes are durable, traceable and visible in all related
    screens.
-   Audit events are complete and do not contain secrets.
-   Desktop, tablet and mobile behavior follows the approved design
    system.

## Screen 36 of 86 --- INV-01: Inventory Overview

### A. Purpose

Provide network-wide visibility of on-hand, reserved, available and
in-transit stock.

### B. Primary users

**Warehouse/Oversight users**

### C. Entry points

-   Relevant role dashboard.
-   Parent module list or work queue.
-   Authorized deep link from a notification, alert, report or linked
    transaction.
-   Related record navigation where the user's scope permits access.

### D. Page layout

-   **Header zone:** breadcrumb, page title, status/context and primary
    action.
-   **Control zone:** search, filters, scope selectors or workflow
    controls as applicable.

    grouped separately; destructive/irreversible actions visually

-   **Responsive behavior:** filters collapse into a drawer on narrow
    screens; tables use priority columns plus expandable row details;
    critical actions remain reachable without horizontal scrolling.

### E. Core fields and data

-   **Warehouse**
-   **Storage location**
-   **Textbook/batch**
-   **Quantity**
-   **Condition**
-   **Stock status**
-   **Source/destination reference**
-   **Movement type**
-   **Date/time**
-   **Responsible officer**

### F. Primary actions

-   **Receive**
-   **Inspect**
-   **Move**
-   **Count**
-   **Reconcile**
-   **Request adjustment**
-   **View ledger**

Open Inventory Overview → resolve role and scope → load authorized data
→ review or enter information → validate → execute permitted action →
persist state change and audit event → update linked screens and
workflow queues.

### H. Business rules

-   On-hand, reserved and available quantities are distinct.
-   No negative inventory is permitted.
-   Adjustments require reason, evidence and approval according to
    threshold.
-   Posted ledger entries are never deleted or edited; corrections use
    reversing entries.
-   The user's effective organizational and geographic scope is checked
    on every read and write operation.
-   A state transition is rejected when prerequisites are incomplete.
-   Historical records referenced by completed transactions cannot be
    silently deleted.
-   Sensitive overrides require a reason and are highlighted for audit
    review.

    constraints.

    scanning and access classification.
-   Concurrent edits use version checks or transaction locking to
    prevent silent overwrites.

-   **Edit:** limited by record state; submitted/approved/posted records

    archive/cancel/reversal workflows.

    sensitivity requires it.
